title: Pulses Grading and Marking Rules 2003
text: Pulses Grading and Marking Rules 1. Short title, animation ud ccnneneeme-t.- (1) These rules may be called the Pulses Gmding and Marking Rules, 2003. (2) They shall apply to whole, hushed and Im‘nusked form of pulses Urd(Phaseolns mungo Linn), Moonga’haseolus aurues chb. or Phaseohrs radiztus Keith), Masooraens mnlenta Moench or Lens mlinan's inedik or Ervum lens Linn), Arhar/Tur(Cajanus cajan), Kabuli chanuCicer arietinrnn Linn), Chana - Bengal grani{Cicer arietinum Linn), Matki/ MothiPhaseolus wonitifolins), Peas(?isum nrvense), Rajmafl’haseolus vulgaris), LobiaWigna sincnsis). (3} They shall come into force from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Definitions.- In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,- (3) "Agricultural Marketing Adviser" means the Agricralnzrnl Marketing Adviser to the Government of India» (b) "Authorised packer" means a person or a body or“ persons who has been granted a certificate of authorisation to grsde and mark the pulses in swordance with the grade standards and procedure prescribed under these rules; (c) "Certificate of Authorisation" means a certificate issued under the provisions of the General Grading and Marking Rules, i988 authorising a person or a body of persons to grade and mark pulses with the grade designation mark; (6) “General Grading and Marking Rules" means the General (lra'a'ing and Marking Rules, I988 made under seen‘rm 3 cfthe Agricultural Produce {Grading and Marking) Act, l937 (l of 1937); (e) "Grade designation mark“ means tile Agmarlr firsignia referred to in rule 5. {0 "Schedule” means a Schedule appended to these rules. 3. Grade deslytafions:- The Grade dnignations :0 indicate the quality of pulses shall he as serum in column I ofSchedule I! to XIXI 4. Quality:— For the purpose of these rules, the quality and gmcral requirements ofpulses shall be as such given agxiust each grade designation from column 2 to 8 of Schedules - II, V, VIII, X,XlI,Xle, XV, XVII, XVIII, XIX and from column 2 to 9 of Schedules HI, EV, VI, VII, 1X. XI, XIV and XVI respectively, 5. Grade designatio- larkp The grade designation mark shall consist of :he design incorporating the certificate of authorisation number, the word 'AGMARK“, name of the commodity and grade designation resembling the one as set out in scheduled. 6, Method of packing (l) Pulses shall be packed in Emmy bags/jute bags, poly woven bags, poly pouches, cloth bags or other suitable packages which shall be clean, sound, flee from insect, fungal infestation and the packin g material shall be as permitted under the Prevention ofFood Adulteraticn Rules, 1955‘ (2) Pulses shall be packed in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutrifioual, technological and organoleptie qualities of the products. (3) The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product. (4) The net weight ofthe pulses in a package shall be 85 per the provision prescribed under the Standards of Weights and Mensrncs (Packaged Commodities) Rules, 1977. (5) Each package shall contain pulses ofthe same type and of the same grade designation (6) Each package shall be securely closed and sealed 7. Method of Markingz- (I) The guide designation marl; shallbesecurely afixed to or printed on each package in a manner approved by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser or an officer authorised by him on his behalfin accordance with rule 11 cfthe General Grading and Marking Rules, I988. 80 u -- 1 of 20 -- (2) In addition to the grade designation mark, following particulars shall he clearly and indelibly marked on each label or packagezn (a) Name and address of the puker; (13) Place ofpacking; (c) Date of packing; (d) Lot/batch number; (e) Net Weight; (0 Grade; (g) Maximum retail Price (inclusive of aii taxes); (is) Best before month year (3} The ink used for marking on packages shall be of such quality which may not contaminate the product (4) The authorised packer, may. alter obtaining prior approval of the Agricultural Marketing Adviser 0r an officer authorised by him on his behaif, mark his private trade mark or trade brand on the graded packages provided that the same do not indicate quaiity other than that indicating by the grade designatian mark affixed to the graded packages in accordance with these rules 8. Special conditions ofeertifieate of authorisation:- In addition to the conditions specified under the sub- mle (8) ot'ruie 3 ofthe Genera} Grading and Marking Rules, 2988, the following shell be the special conditions ofevery certificate of amhorization issued for the purpose ofthese rules:- (i) The authorised packer shail either set up his own iaborslcry or have access to an approved grading llboratory fer testing of Pulses. (ii) The premises shaii be maintained in perfect hygienic and sanitary mnditimrs with proper ventilaflbns and we“ lighted arrangement. The personals engaged in these operations shall be in sound health and free from any infectious, contagious or communicable diseases (iii) The premises shai’. have adequate storage fianilities with pucca floor and free fiom rodent and insect infestation, {iv) The antherised packer shall observe ail instructions regarding iesling, grading, packing, marking, sealing and main‘senance of recs: 5 which may be issued from time to time by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser or any other officer authfirised by him in this behalf ‘ scnmum—i (See rule 5) (Design ()ngka INSlGNIA) {picture as depicted on page 50 (Schedule I1 81 if -- 2 of 20 -- SCHEDULE-II (See rules 3 and 4) Grade designation and definition of quality of Urd whole (Bllck gram) Grade Definition of quality Designation Special Requirements Maximum limits oftolerance (per cent by weight) Moisture Foreign matter Other ediBF‘n‘Emaged Weevilled ------------- grains Grains grains per cent by count Organic finmrg‘anic 7 7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 Special 100 0.10 Nil 0.1 0.5 2.0 Standard 12.0 0.50 0.10 0.5 2.0 4.0 General 14.0 0.75 0.25 3.0 5.0 6.0 Note.- In foreign matter, the impurities of animal origin shall not be more than 0.10 per cent by weight.
